references.text: [EFRS93] Erd\H{o}s, Paul and Faudree, R. J. and Rousseau, C. C. and Schelp, R. H., Ramsey size linear graphs. Combin. Probab. Comput. (1993), 389-399.

remarks.text:
In other words, is $G$ Ramsey size linear? This fails for a graph $G$ with $n$ vertices and $2n-2$ edges (for example with $H=K_n$). Erd\H{o}s, Faudree, Rousseau, and Schelp \cite{EFRS93} have shown that any graph $G$ with $n$ vertices and at most $n+1$ edges is Ramsey size linear.

Implies [567].

This problem is #31 in Ramsey Theory in the graphs problem collection.

statement.text: Let $G$ be such that any subgraph on $k$ vertices has at most $2k-3$ edges. Is it true that, if $H$ has $m$ edges and no isolated vertices, then\[R(G,H)\ll m?\]
